118197.explanation: We strongly support resident influence on municipal policy, but a referendum is usually not the right method.

118198.explanation: If you give people the trust to build something together in their own neighborhood, beautiful things arise. The municipality should not hinder this but rather encourage it.

118200.explanation: We are open to longer opening hours, but in practice this is difficult because more nighttime police deployment would be required.

118202.explanation: In the Netherlands, we do not abandon anyone. If we offer help, rejected asylum seekers should cooperate with their return when possible. This is, however, mainly a problem in the large cities.

118204.explanation: This is bad for health and the environment. Banning it is not a high priority for us.

118209.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. But many important places are still not well accessible, for example for wheelchair users. Therefore, we believe the municipality should step in if truly necessary.

118215.explanation: This was already agreed upon in the last budget.

118217.explanation: The safety of victims is the most important. But it is always preferred that the perpetrator is removed from the home, if that can be done safely.

118219.explanation: This has been the municipality's policy for years.

118220.explanation: The waiting lists are still too long for residents of Lelystad.

118223.explanation: We support converting vacant commercial buildings, as is already happening a lot in the city center. But this can usually be done well without subsidies.

118227.explanation: It is more important that people find the municipal schemes. So simpler rules and better communication.

118228.explanation: Youth work is an important preventive activity. Without youth work there would be more problems such as school dropout, crime, and other nuisance. Ultimately, that would also be more expensive.

118236.explanation: Price is important, but quality is more important.

118237.explanation: At specific locations and for specific target groups.

118239.explanation: The dog tax is one of the municipality's incomes. If this is abolished, other taxes must be raised.

118240.explanation: In principle, the tourist tax is high enough. However, we believe the municipality should make more effort to actually collect the tourist tax.

118242.explanation: In principle, we support leaving this completely free. However, the customer base is currently too small for entrepreneurs to run a profitable business. So it is fine that it is now once a month.

118245.explanation: But preferably solar panels on roofs.

118246.explanation: Theater is essential for a vibrant city.

118247.explanation: That would be nice, but swimming pools are very expensive. Hopefully this will be possible in the future, but not in the coming years.

118248.explanation: If schools want, it must be possible.

118249.explanation: A second coffeeshop is possible for us.

118250.explanation: We also want to create mixed neighborhoods in new construction with all types of housing. But in Lelystad there is a desperate shortage of affordable rental homes, so we want to build many of those.

118253.explanation: Parking in Lelystad is very cheap and the municipality is currently making a loss on this.

118255.explanation: As it is now, it is good: car-reduced.

118256.explanation: This decision lies with the national government.

118257.explanation: In an ideal world we would want mixed neighborhoods. But the housing shortage is so great now that labor migrants are crammed into too-small living spaces, causing much nuisance for the neighborhood. Therefore, at this moment we prefer adequate housing elsewhere.

118197.explanation: For us as VVD, the starting point is that the Lelystad Council represents the residents. Moreover, the Environment and Planning Act requires participation with the environment for new initiatives. This must be organized by the initiator themselves. As far as we are concerned, this suffices.

118198.explanation: Taking over management of small green spaces is possible, but for good quality public spaces it is important that the municipality manages them. Sports facilities are managed by Sportbedrijf or associations. The sale of small green spaces adjacent to private property can be explored.

118200.explanation: As liberals, we stand for our entrepreneurs. We want to end unnecessary barriers to entrepreneurship in our city. So grant permits on time and extend where possible. For hospitality permits: always in coordination with regulations and emergency services to guarantee safety.

118202.explanation: No asylum seekers center in Lelystad. Lelystad meets the housing task for status holders. There is no room for an asylum seekers center in our municipality. We maintain the exemption our city received from the cabinet.

118203.explanation: Where forest compensation is mandatory, this must be completed, but a strict obligation to replace every cut tree with a new one goes too far.

118204.explanation: Individual freedom is our top priority. If fireplaces and wood stoves comply with national guidelines, they may be used freely.

118209.explanation: We consider it very important that buildings in Lelystad are well accessible, but the municipality's responsibility is limited to municipal buildings ('social real estate'). Our election program also states that sports should be accessible to everyone.

118215.explanation: From our election program: 1. The capacity of enforcement and emergency services in Lelystad must be maintained. 2. Enforcement officers with the right equipment. Batons, pepper spray, and bodycams increase safety for both enforcers and residents.

118217.explanation: Femicide and violence against women is an increasing societal problem. Safety comes first. A safe (temporary) home for this vulnerable group is more than just a signal. The victim should expect support from the government.

118219.explanation: The existing starter loan must be maintained and, where necessary, the available budget (loan ceiling) should be responsibly increased by the Lelystad Council.

118220.explanation: We do not want one-sided neighborhoods, but a diverse city with a healthy balance between different housing types for various target groups, with shops and other facilities, and accessible for our residents.

118223.explanation: We support transforming office buildings and vacant retail properties into housing, as has already been done successfully in the City Center. This should not be realized with municipal subsidies but by initiators such as entrepreneurs or cooperatives based on a solid business case.

118227.explanation: The existing income threshold in Lelystad’s poverty policy suffices.

118228.explanation: Preventive youth work prevents young people from slipping into crime. We do critically examine the actual effects of youth work.

118236.explanation: Specialized youth care is purchased regionally together with other municipalities to keep costs low while delivering good quality. Cost control in youth care is not the starting point in general, but the result of policy and execution by providers.

118237.explanation: Combating period poverty is part of Lelystad's poverty policy, and this must remain so. Free menstrual products are distributed through schools. As VVD, we supported the motion calling for this on December 21, 2021.

118239.explanation: The dog tax is not a targeted tax. If we were to abolish it, we would need to find budget coverage elsewhere (and thus indicate what we will no longer do).

118240.explanation: Recreation and tourism are important for Lelystad. We wholeheartedly support recreational businesses that want to operate in Lelystad. Increasing tourist tax does not fit with this.

118242.explanation: Entrepreneurs who want to must always be able to open their stores on Sundays. This applies not only to Bataviastad but also to entrepreneurs elsewhere in the city.

118245.explanation: A total of 1,000 hectares of solar installations on land are allowed in the province of Flevoland, as laid down in the provincial Solar Structure Vision. We adhere to the principles in this policy, but farmers must have the space to install solar panels on land if it fits within the policy.

118246.explanation: In recent years, the Agora Theatre has succeeded in becoming financially healthy through good cultural entrepreneurship. A contribution in the form of cultural subsidy is indispensable, but the current subsidy amount (€1.35 million per year) is sufficient.

118247.explanation: The De Koploper swimming pool is easily accessible from Zuiderhage and has sufficient capacity.

118248.explanation: In a water-rich city like Lelystad, a swimming diploma is necessary. Swimming proficiency is a must for every child to feel safe near water. Swimming also benefits exercise standards and a healthy lifestyle.

118249.explanation: The existing coffeeshop has sufficient capacity to meet demand. Soft drugs are tolerated; they are not legalized products and are demonstrably addictive as various studies show. Also, soft drugs demonstrably lead to more crime.

118250.explanation: We adhere to the 'Lelystad mix' from the Housing Vision: a maximum of 30% of new-build homes may be social rental housing. Social rental homes are only available to those who need them most. Therefore, we tackle mismatched housing.

118253.explanation: From our election program: The burdens for our residents remain as low as possible; unnecessary tax increases are non-negotiable for us.

118254.explanation: Where possible, we want more parking space in the city center. Therefore, when developing new residential complexes in the City Center, sufficient indoor and structured parking must be created for residents and visitors, based on realistic parking standards for the city center.

118255.explanation: From our election program: Anyone who wants to can park in the City Center. Everyone who wishes to come to the City Center by car must be able to find a parking spot there, both residents and visitors.

118256.explanation: Lelystad Airport must be open for holiday flights. Stationing F35s at the airport is only acceptable if Lelystad Airport finally opens for commercial traffic (= holiday flights).

118257.explanation: From our election program: Labor migrants have adequate housing on the company site where they work or in specially built and commercially developed locations on the outskirts of the city.

118197.explanation: Agree, JongLelystad finds active participation of residents extremely important; therefore, residents should be able to directly influence important decisions that affect the city.

118198.explanation: Agree, because residents often know better what their neighborhood needs. More say leads to more involvement and better facilities.

118200.explanation: Agree, because entrepreneurs know best what works for their business and their guests. More freedom here provides a more attractive city life for our youth and young adults. It is important, however, that this does not lead to excessive nuisance for neighbors.

118202.explanation: Neutral. It is important to treat people already accommodated in Lelystad humanely. At the same time, the municipality does not necessarily have to organize additional shelter automatically, as this can place pressure on facilities, capacity, and livability. Each situation requires careful consideration.

118203.explanation: Agree, because we want our children to grow up in a green and healthy city. What we cut down today, we must replant for the future and future generations.

118204.explanation: Agree, because freedom of choice in one’s own home is important. With clear agreements, the use of fireplaces and wood stoves can be responsibly maintained without unnecessary nuisance in the neighborhood.

118209.explanation: Agree, because accessibility often involves tailored solutions and high costs and is therefore not automatically addressed. With targeted support, the municipality can help entrepreneurs invest in a city that is accessible to residents with disabilities.

118215.explanation: Agree, safety is increasingly becoming a problem in Lelystad. Our residents must feel safe in their city. More visible enforcement officers provide prevention, faster intervention, and a better living climate in Lelystad.

118217.explanation: Agree, because everyone (especially women and children) has the right to a safe place to flee domestic violence. With such a facility, Lelystad offers protection and a new start for these residents.

118219.explanation: Agree, because starters and young families hardly stand a chance on the housing market without support. A starter loan helps them buy their first home and stay living in Lelystad.

118220.explanation: Agree, because Lelystad residents, especially youth and young families, have to wait too long for a social rental home. Priority for local residents helps them continue living here and build a future.

118223.explanation: Agree, because vacant offices can quickly produce housing. A subsidy can help bridge the high renovation costs and make housing projects feasible (which otherwise would not be viable).

118227.explanation: Agree, unfortunately there is significant poverty in Lelystad. By raising the income threshold, we prevent worsening problems and people falling through the cracks.

118228.explanation: Disagree, because youth work is essential for guidance, prevention, and opportunities for young people. Cutting back here causes greater problems in the long term.

118236.explanation: Disagree, because quality and stability must be the priority in youth care. Selecting solely based on price compromises appropriate care (and exacerbates problems).

118237.explanation: Agree, because access to female hygiene products should be taken for granted. Providing them for free prevents inequality and ensures everyone can continue to participate.

118239.explanation: Disagree, because the dog tax helps cover costs for maintenance and supervision. However, we believe the revenues must genuinely be used for this purpose. Also, as an exception, we believe lonely elderly should be eligible for a dog tax waiver.

118240.explanation: Disagree, because a higher tourist tax makes Lelystad less attractive to visitors. Tourism actually brings vitality and income to local entrepreneurs and the city.

118242.explanation: Agree, because additional Sunday shopping days make the shopping center more attractive and give residents more freedom to shop whenever it suits them. This helps make Lelystad more appealing.

118245.explanation: Disagree, we are not necessarily opposed. If this can be lucrative for the owners of agricultural land (farmers), it could be a consideration. Moreover, it helps with sustainability.

118246.explanation: Disagree, because without financial support it is difficult for cultural institutions to survive. This is a general problem, but especially so in Lelystad.

118247.explanation: Agree, because Lelystad currently has only one swimming pool located mostly in the northeast. A second pool in Zuiderhage improves distribution, gives residents more choice, and can encourage new and better facilities through healthy competition.

118248.explanation: Agree, because learning to swim is essential for children's safety and development, especially in a "water city" like Lelystad. Municipal support ensures no child is excluded.

118249.explanation: Agree, Lelystad already has one coffeeshop which suffices for current demand. More coffeeshops add little and can unnecessarily put pressure on the livability and safety of the city.

118250.explanation: Agree, because Lelystad has a relatively large group of low and middle incomes. With a minimum of 30% social housing, we better align with the city's demographics and keep housing affordable for starters and young families.

118253.explanation: Disagree, because higher parking fees drive people away from the city center (Stadshart). The center is already not very attractive. An attractive and accessible center requires affordable parking.

118254.explanation: Neutral, partly disagree because in general there are enough parking spaces, but partly agree since some locations need more parking, such as near Jumbo/BasicFit or Billy's.

118255.explanation: Disagree, because a car-free city center hinders daily use and accessibility for many residents. Accessibility and vibrancy must remain balanced, especially in a city like Lelystad which is naturally quite spaciously designed.

118256.explanation: Agree, because vacation flights can generate economic growth and jobs for Lelystad. This is an opportunity for Lelystad to truly grow and can also contribute to strong regional development.

118257.explanation: Neutral, partly agree because one large residential location can help reduce exploitation and nuisance in neighborhoods, but partly disagree because large-scale housing is only acceptable if good care, guidance, and facilities are arranged nearby.

118197.explanation: The municipal council is elected as the people's representative and should make decisions in the interest of the city and its residents.

118198.explanation: In Lelystad we have the 'right to challenge,' whereby it is important that if residents take over management of something, the social added value is considered, such as strengthening social cohesion and stimulating encounters.

118200.explanation: No, citizens must be protected against unnecessary noise nuisance.

118202.explanation: Someone who has undergone the process and was rejected is improperly using the facilities.

118203.explanation: It is important that this is done on municipal land itself to preserve a green Lelystad.

118204.explanation: Wood stoves and fireplaces can cause nuisance. Responsible burning is a principle here, for example not burning when there is no wind.

118209.explanation: An entrepreneur determines the hospitality of their own building.

118215.explanation: Safety of the people of Lelystad is important and the enforcement officers contribute to this.

118217.explanation: It is important that there is a women's shelter, especially for those who need an extra safe place.

118220.explanation: It is important to reduce waiting lists and continue to engage young people with our city.

118223.explanation: The municipality should not provide subsidies but should cooperate with such plans.

118227.explanation: It must fit within the (financial) possibilities of the municipality.

118228.explanation: Youth work ensures fewer young people ultimately end up in youth care. Additionally, it offers perspective to the youth of Lelystad.

118236.explanation: There must be a sharp balance between the price and quality of care.

118237.explanation: Thanks to an initiative from the municipal council, this is already provided free of charge at schools.

118239.explanation: In Lelystad we determine the dog policy and the associated maintenance.

118240.explanation: Tourist tax has recently been introduced and must be cost-covering.

118246.explanation: Stimulating culture is important for the welfare of the people of Lelystad.

118247.explanation: Lelystad will grow to over 100,000 inhabitants in the coming years, and new facilities must correspond to this.

118248.explanation: Every child is proficient in swimming by the end of primary school age.

118250.explanation: In Lelystad, we have the Lelystad mix, which includes this topic to achieve a balanced distribution.

118253.explanation: Parking costs should be cost-covering; the municipality does not need to profit from this.

118255.explanation: It must primarily be safe, and the infrastructure should be adjusted accordingly.

118256.explanation: Yes, with a commitment to sustainable aviation.

118257.explanation: To prevent problems like those in the Bastion.

118197.explanation: Participation is good, but only if the municipality cannot easily ignore it.

118198.explanation: We have seen before that it eventually does not work. This is due to various reasons: changes in management, too few volunteers, etc.

118202.explanation: If asylum seekers are rejected, they must return to their own country. That is the law.

118203.explanation: We have a lot of nature and we must preserve it.

118209.explanation: There is already policy and subsidies for that.

118215.explanation: We want to allocate a budget for that.

118217.explanation: This has already been arranged regionally.

118223.explanation: Sometimes it is good to convert vacant offices, and sometimes it is better to demolish them.

118227.explanation: The income limit does not need to be raised, but the municipality must better communicate about existing schemes.

118228.explanation: We do consider youth work important.

118236.explanation: The most important thing is a good price-quality ratio.

118237.explanation: Is already implemented now, also in schools.

118239.explanation: Dog owners currently contribute 680,000 euros to the general fund. We consider this an unfair tax.

118240.explanation: We are happy with all tourists who stay overnight in Lelystad.

118242.explanation: Shops are already allowed to open every Sunday in Lelystad.

118245.explanation: First investigate other possibilities. Many more solar panels can still be placed on roofs.

118247.explanation: There is a great need for an outdoor swimming pool.

118248.explanation: Not financially feasible, but for parents who cannot afford swimming lessons, there is a subsidy available from NOC-NSF.

118249.explanation: There is already a very nice facility.

118250.explanation: That is already stated in the Lelystad housing vision.

118253.explanation: There is no need for that in our city.

118254.explanation: There are enough parking spaces in the city center.

118257.explanation: As long as there is no nuisance in the neighborhoods.

118197.explanation: No referendum but definitely want to participate in discussions.

118198.explanation: The given examples vary considerably. Adopting a park should certainly be possible; the operation of the sports hall should preferably be assigned to the Sports Company.

118200.explanation: Equal closing times ensure equal competitive conditions.

118202.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers are not welcome.

118203.explanation: Preserve green areas due to climate adaptation.

118204.explanation: The Residents' Party believes that the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves in Lelystad should remain permitted, provided this is done within existing environmental and health standards.

118209.explanation: The entrepreneur must cover this cost themselves - a fixed condition for the permit.

118215.explanation: Especially in the evenings and on weekends. Also giving their work a different form.

118219.explanation: A starter loan helps people buying a house for the first time to lower the financial threshold and thus increase accessibility to the housing market.

118223.explanation: Give project developers space for this.

118228.explanation: The municipality must not cut funding for youth work because this is essential for providing support, prevention, and opportunities to young people, thereby preventing social problems and strengthening the livability of the neighborhood.

118236.explanation: Price does not always mean better; it is about the process to be followed.

118237.explanation: In cooperation with the poverty fund at schools.

118242.explanation: No need for this in Lelystad.

118245.explanation: Where there is room, this should be possible. It has no priority.

118246.explanation: Agora has already received a lot of extra subsidies in the past. It’s time they stand on their own feet.

118248.explanation: Through the sports and culture fund for people on welfare, others pay themselves.

118250.explanation: 30% social housing is set by the municipal council.

118253.explanation: Our center currently has little to offer residents. Make it more attractive for our residents first.

118256.explanation: Is good for the local, regional economy.

118257.explanation: On the edge of the city.

118197.explanation: For important issues with major consequences for Lelystad, it is good that residents can express their opinion. A referendum can be an appropriate tool alongside the role of the municipal council.

118198.explanation: Residents and associations can play a valuable role in managing facilities, provided this is voluntary and well organized. The municipality remains responsible for safety, quality, and continuity and must not simply shift tasks.

118200.explanation: Entrepreneurs know best when they want to be open. The municipality should give them that space within existing rules for safety and livability. Less unnecessary interference, more trust in entrepreneurs.

118202.explanation: Those who have been rejected have no right to shelter. Continuing to house rejected asylum seekers undermines asylum policy and increases pressure on housing, care, and safety. Lelystad should not bear the burden of national failures.

118203.explanation: Trees are important, but a strict one-to-one obligation is too rigid. Replanting is desirable where possible, but customized solutions are needed due to safety, space, and costs. No symbolic rules, but sensible choices per situation.

118204.explanation: Residents should be able to decide how they heat their homes. Bans on open fireplaces and wood stoves are patronizing and mainly affect people at home. You address nuisance in a targeted way, without general bans.

118209.explanation: Accessibility is important, but it is not obvious that the municipality should always pay for it. Entrepreneurs are primarily responsible for their own buildings. The municipality can support or think along where needed but should not introduce a generic financial scheme.

118215.explanation: Safety comes first. More visible enforcement on the streets creates calm and a safer feeling in neighborhoods. Community officers (Boa’s) are an important addition to the police and should mainly focus on nuisance and public order.

118217.explanation: Victims of domestic violence deserve immediate protection. A shelter is necessary for safety in emergency situations. The focus must be on protecting victims and tackling perpetrators, with clear rules and temporary accommodation.

118219.explanation: Starters are finding it increasingly difficult in the housing market. A starter loan can help take the first step towards a home purchase. This must be targeted and within clear conditions so it remains affordable and does not have a price-inflating effect.

118220.explanation: Residents who live here and contribute have been on the waiting list for years. It is logical and fair that they get priority for social rental homes. Lelystad homes should primarily be available to Lelystad residents.

118223.explanation: Converting vacant offices into homes can be a good solution, but this does not have to be standard with municipal subsidy. Market parties should take the lead first. The municipality can facilitate but should not hand out blank checks.

118227.explanation: Poverty aid must reach people who really need it. Raising the income limit makes schemes too broad and less fair. Targeted help and customization are better than automatically broadening limits.

118228.explanation: Youth work must be effective and produce results. The municipality needs to critically examine what works and what doesn’t. Money that has no demonstrable effect on safety, perspective, and participation of youth should be used differently or better.

118236.explanation: In youth care, quality and effectiveness must always come first. Focusing only on price leads to poorer care and higher costs in the long term. The municipality must choose what works for the child, not the cheapest provider.

118237.explanation: No one should get into trouble because of poverty, but the municipality handing out free products is not a structural solution. Help should be provided through existing poverty policies and tailored support, not through general free provisions.

118239.explanation: The dog tax is an unfair extra burden for pet owners and is unrelated to actual costs. Responsible dog owners should not be penalized with a separate tax. This tax can be abolished.

118240.explanation: A higher tourist tax makes Lelystad less attractive to visitors and harms local businesses. Tourism should contribute to the local economy, not be discouraged by higher charges. Raising taxes is not the solution.

118242.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be able to decide their opening hours, and residents when to shop. More shopping Sundays increase freedom of choice and strengthen the local economy. No one is forced; those who want to remain closed can stay closed.

118245.explanation: Agricultural land is meant for food production, not for large-scale solar parks. Solar panels belong on roofs, industrial sites and existing buildings. We must protect our scarce farmland and not sacrifice valuable space to symbolic policies.

118246.explanation: Theater Agora fulfills a cultural function, but subsidy is not automatic. The municipality must strictly manage costs, programming, and reach, and only contribute where it demonstrably adds value for Lelystad. No blank check, but critical supervision.

118247.explanation: Lelystad is growing and one swimming pool is insufficient for sports, water safety, and recreation. A second swimming pool is needed for the future. During the development of Zuiderhage this is a logical moment, provided it is realized affordably and efficiently.

118248.explanation: School swimming is important for children’s safety. In a water-rich country like the Netherlands, every child must learn to swim. The municipality has a responsibility here and must ensure schools can offer this.

118249.explanation: Coffeeshops cause nuisance, crime, and an attracting effect. Lelystad already has one and we do not want new ones, preferably eventually zero. The municipality must focus on safety and livability, not on expanding drug sales.

118250.explanation: The housing shortage is severe, especially for people with lower incomes. New construction must include enough social rental housing. A guideline of at least 30% is defensible, as long as this does not lead to one-sided neighborhoods and there remains room for middle-income and owner-occupied homes.

118253.explanation: More expensive parking drives visitors and customers away from the center and affects residents with cars. The center must remain accessible for everyone. Parking must not become a cash cow for the municipality.

118254.explanation: The center must remain easily accessible for residents and visitors. Additional parking spaces reduce parking pressure and search traffic and support entrepreneurs. Accessibility is essential for a lively center.

118255.explanation: A car-free center makes Lelystad less accessible for residents, the elderly, and entrepreneurs. Visitors must be able to reach the center easily, including by car. Accessibility and livability are more important than harassing car drivers.

118256.explanation: Holiday flights from Lelystad Airport create jobs, economic growth, and relieve Schiphol. This is possible, provided safety and livability are guaranteed and agreements on nuisance and routes are respected. This way Lelystad benefits without unnecessary disturbance.

118257.explanation: Large housing locations for migrant workers put extra pressure on the housing market, facilities, and livability. Lelystad must focus on homes for its own residents. Work should not automatically lead to large-scale housing of migrant workers.

118197.explanation: We support advisory referendums if the resident initiates them.

118198.explanation: Let the sports hall management remain with the sports company; green spaces can be very well managed by residents (very successful).

118200.explanation: Under predetermined conditions, entrepreneurs should be able to determine extended opening hours themselves.

118202.explanation: A fair, just and sustainable asylum policy means that people fleeing war and violence receive the protection they deserve. It also means that those who do not have the right to asylum must leave the Netherlands.

118203.explanation: We must remain a green municipality, planting trees back in Lelystad.

118204.explanation: In our spacious city this must remain possible, especially with high electricity and gas prices.

118209.explanation: Entrepreneurs bear the costs themselves; help is available through Stichting GOL.

118215.explanation: Our preference is for more police on the streets.

118217.explanation: If necessary, we support a women's shelter.

118219.explanation: The current arrangement must be maintained.

118220.explanation: Residents of Lelystad do not get priority for social rental housing; professions important to the city (regardless of residence) should get priority, such as police officers, nurses, and teachers.

118223.explanation: No subsidy should be granted for this.

118227.explanation: As soon as it is necessary to combat poverty.

118228.explanation: Young people are the future! More attention is needed for preventing problems so that demand for help is lower.

118236.explanation: The family is central. The right care in the right place.

118237.explanation: The municipality can provide subsidies (money) to organizations to distribute it.

118239.explanation: Used to maintain facilities for dogs; if abolished, the property tax (OZB) must be increased.

118242.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be able to determine their own opening hours.

118245.explanation: No solar panels on our good farmland.

118246.explanation: The theater is very important for the city and must continue to exist.

118247.explanation: In combination with the new sports park in that neighborhood, we should definitely consider an outdoor pool.

118248.explanation: We live in a water-rich environment, swimming lessons save lives. We are willing to spend money on this.

118249.explanation: Lelystad is a large municipality; a second coffeeshop can prevent illegal sales. We do support legal cannabis cultivation.

118250.explanation: The current mix between social housing and other housing for new construction projects must be maintained on average.

118253.explanation: The increase in parking fees should be limited to an inflation adjustment; the second hour of parking is currently free.

118254.explanation: With the growth of the city, additional parking facilities should be considered on the edge of the west side of the center.

118255.explanation: The center must remain accessible by car, but we do support fewer cars in the center.

118256.explanation: Lelystad Airport must be open for passenger airplanes.

118257.explanation: A start has already been made here, and it can help to make homes available in the residential neighborhoods.

118198.explanation: Management of playgrounds in the neighborhood is acceptable, but the management of a sports hall must remain in professional hands.

118200.explanation: Mooi Lelystad gives entrepreneurs free rein regarding opening hours.

118202.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers must be deported, and this must be punishable by law.

118203.explanation: Mooi Lelystad supports tree compensation. For every tree cut down, one tree must be planted.

118204.explanation: Use of fireplaces remains permitted. However, residents should be urged to refrain during heavy fog.

118209.explanation: All buildings and public spaces must be accessible to everyone.

118215.explanation: Safety in the neighborhood is a basic provision. If extra BOA (official neighborhood enforcement officers) capacity is needed to reduce nuisance and crime, that must be possible, even if it costs money. Explanation: You must feel safe in your own neighborhood. With more BOAs, we can intervene faster and stop nuisance sooner.

118217.explanation: Given the increasing violent incidents against women, such a shelter is certainly necessary.

118219.explanation: Not a task for the municipality.

118220.explanation: Residents of Lelystad must have a fair chance at housing in their own city. Local ties and long waiting times should count towards priority. Explanation: Those who live, work, and contribute here deserve a fair chance. This way, we keep Lelystad accessible for our own residents.

118223.explanation: Contributes to solving the housing shortage.

118228.explanation: Youth care is a core task and must continue. If the budget is too tight, we must reconsider other expenses, preferably by cutting inefficiencies and projects with little effect. Explanation: Children must receive timely help. If necessary, we will postpone other (non-essential) expenditures.

118236.explanation: The price-quality ratio must be balanced.

118237.explanation: In summer, sun protection devices may also be added.

118239.explanation: Dog tax is an unfair fine on a pet. We will abolish the dog tax and opt for a municipality that is fair and predictable in taxation, without extra charges for dog owners.

118240.explanation: Tourists may well contribute a bit towards our amenities.

118242.explanation: Opening hours are determined by the entrepreneurs. We leave this open.

118245.explanation: We have the cleanest farmland in the Netherlands. Mooi Lelystad wants to keep it that way. Solar panels can be placed on roofs, dikes, along highways, etc.

118246.explanation: We are against structural dependency in the cultural sector. If a new pop venue is built, it must have strong own income and sharp choices within the budget. Explanation: We opt for affordability and smart investments, not higher burdens for everyone.

118247.explanation: Prefer a deep investment in a swimming pool for everyone rather than a new pop venue with heavy investments.

118248.explanation: Especially in a watery area like Flevoland.

118249.explanation: We have a coffeeshop, and we find that sufficient.

118250.explanation: With a mix of social rental, mid-range rental, and affordable purchase, neighborhoods remain livable and starters, families, and seniors have real choices.

118253.explanation: Parking in the center should encourage people to stay longer. Therefore, we primarily want to lower the rate for longer parking to make the center more attractive.

118254.explanation: At least 1 parking space per home or shop.

118255.explanation: That will be at the expense of our entrepreneurs in the center.

118256.explanation: Investments have been made with the goal of opening Lelystad Airport for civil aviation (holiday flights). We will ensure that this promise is fulfilled; otherwise, compensation from the national government is due to Lelystad. After all, holiday flights mean jobs and a boost for the city.

118257.explanation: Prefer well-managed locations for housing labor migrants at the edge of the city rather than spread in residential neighborhoods, but under strict conditions for registration, management, and livability.

118197.explanation: Our residents must be much better involved in matters the city council decides on. The referendum is a good tool for this. This possibility already exists in Lelystad but is currently hardly used.

118198.explanation: We are strong supporters of collective solutions: neighborhoods that come together and decide for themselves what happens in their area. Joint management of, for example, a sports hall or a park fits this well.

118200.explanation: Our entrepreneurs are crucial to what we want to achieve in Lelystad: a livelier city center with more nightlife options, also in the evening/night. We must give local entrepreneurs space, but of course with consideration for the surroundings.

118202.explanation: Lelystad currently hosts an above-average number of asylum seekers. It is unfair if the pressure on our city is further increased while wealthy municipalities consistently host fewer people.

118203.explanation: We have enough space in the polder for more greenery. We must preserve our beautiful green areas and expand them where possible. If trees need to be cut down, this must not lead to less overall greenery in the municipality.

118204.explanation: Ordinary households with fireplaces or wood stoves contribute relatively little to pollution, while the main polluters are elsewhere. The municipality can practically not completely ban this, and it would be almost impossible to enforce such a ban.

118209.explanation: There should be more to do in Lelystad, for everyone. Therefore, our City Center and neighborhood centers must be accessible to people with disabilities. Not every entrepreneur can afford these costs themselves, so it is important that the municipality provides financial support.

118215.explanation: Enforcers are essential for tackling nuisance, and it is a fact that Lelystad currently has far too few. That is why the SP is committed to having more enforcers on the streets.

118217.explanation: Femicide, domestic violence, and stalking are terrible problems that thankfully are receiving more attention. The SP wants Lelystad to take the lead in combating these issues, for example by establishing a women's shelter or using tools such as the 'victim device.'

118219.explanation: To give first-time buyers a chance in the housing market, it is important that Lelystad continues to provide starter loans. Additionally, building more social rental homes remains a top priority for the SP so that young people can better cope with the housing crisis.

118220.explanation: People from Lelystad must be able to build a future in their own city. Currently, young people are often driven away due to the lack of this possibility. That is why youth policy is the number one priority for the SP: more activities in Lelystad, attracting higher education, and also realizing affordable housing.

118223.explanation: The first thing train travelers from the Randstad see when entering Lelystad is the old Getronics building, which has deteriorated greatly after more than a decade of vacancy. The municipality must take active measures when such buildings are vacant and turn them into housing.

118227.explanation: We do not see this as an effective way to actually reduce poverty. The municipality must implement accessible policies where officials are in direct contact with residents who need help. The SP has successfully introduced this approach in several municipalities.

118228.explanation: Youth work is extremely valuable for our city as a form of prevention. Under no circumstances should this be cut. It would be very illogical: for the money we invest in youth workers, we ultimately get more in return.

118236.explanation: The most important thing is that our youth have access to high-quality care. The waiting lists for youth care in Lelystad are currently unacceptably long.

118237.explanation: Period poverty is a problem that deserves attention and must be combated as much as possible. The food bank currently already provides free female hygiene products. This must continue.

118239.explanation: Local taxes, especially the dog tax, must be minimized as much as possible.

118240.explanation: By raising the tourist tax, Lelystad can generate additional income that directly benefits the city. This could, for example, make it possible to reduce or abolish the dog tax.

118242.explanation: More shopping Sundays create extra vibrancy in the City Center (which is desperately needed) and give entrepreneurs the chance to increase their income. Sunday is no longer just a day of rest.

118245.explanation: Solar energy and wind energy are the energies of the future. Lelystad must strive to lead in this area. A ban on solar panels on agricultural land absolutely does not fit with this.

118246.explanation: A lively city center is a top priority for the SP. This includes investments in culture, such as subsidizing the Agora theater and building a new pop venue next to it.

118247.explanation: Currently, there is far too little to do in Lelystad, which is why the SP supports various initiatives to expand the range of experiences. However, we already have a swimming pool, so we find it difficult to see the added value of a second one.

118248.explanation: Every child must have the chance to learn to swim.

118249.explanation: By making cannabis legally available, we ensure safe products of good quality and reduce the nuisance and crime associated with illegal trade. That is why we want the coffeeshop in Lelystad to participate in the national cannabis experiment as soon as possible.

118250.explanation: The SP goes a step further: at least 40% of the new homes built must be social rental homes. This is necessary to address the significant waiting lists in our city and give young people a chance to build a future here.

118253.explanation: Parking must be as cheap as possible for our residents.

118254.explanation: The problem is that our city center is currently not very attractive to visit. Existing parking garages are often very empty, which means more parking spaces are not needed at the moment.

118255.explanation: The center is already car-free enough. Almost only entrepreneurs and residents drive there.

118256.explanation: We are not in favor of opening Lelystad Airport for holiday flights. We find it unacceptable to expose Lelystad to the nuisance of unnecessary air traffic.

118257.explanation: Large residential locations for migrant workers, with sufficient facilities and enforcement, are a valid alternative to the current situation. Subletting, as in Bastion, is often illegal, unsustainable, and causes a lot of nuisance in several neighborhoods.

118197.explanation: It is clear in our program: we will introduce binding local referenda so that residents can directly express their opinions on important decisions. Returning the municipality to the citizens: less backroom deals, more democracy.

118198.explanation: We want to give the municipality back to the citizens. By cutting bureaucracy and giving room to neighborhood initiatives, we strengthen social cohesion. Residents themselves know best what their neighborhood needs. Trust in the power of society, not the civil service.

118200.explanation: We stand for entrepreneurial freedom and a vibrant local economy. Hospitality entrepreneurs should be able to decide for themselves when to open their doors without bureaucratic obstacles. Fewer rules and more space for retailers makes Lelystad lively.

118202.explanation: Local facilities are for our own residents, not for those who reside here illegally. We oppose any form of (emergency) shelter and sever ties with migration organizations. Rejected means leaving; no reward for illegality at the expense of the citizen.

118203.explanation: We refuse rigid rules that unnecessarily delay and make housing and infrastructure more expensive. We choose active greening where it makes sense, not bureaucratic replanting obligations. No ideological symbolic politics, but a practical view of public spaces.

118204.explanation: We stand for absolute freedom in energy choice and oppose any ban on burning. Wood stoves are part of our traditions and provide independence from the energy grid. No ideological coercion or patronizing in the living room; the resident decides for themselves.

118209.explanation: We believe in entrepreneurial freedom and personal responsibility, not new subsidy streams. The municipality should lower burdens for entrepreneurs (property taxes) and cut rules so they have the space to invest in their premises and accessibility.

118215.explanation: Neither. We want more safety on the streets, but also a clear separation between police and enforcement officers. Enforcement officers should focus on their core tasks and not become an 'alternative police.' We call for more real police officers visible in neighborhoods, not simply more enforcement officers.

118217.explanation: Safety for victims is crucial, but a 'women's shelter' requires firm guarantees: location, security, management, costs, and avoiding nuisance in residential areas. First a clear plan and conditions, then a decision.

118219.explanation: No subsidies or starter loans that drive up prices and push starters deeper into debt. We choose more flow-through (senior housing, life-course-proof, multigenerational living) and more supply through fewer rules. This creates a healthier housing market.

118220.explanation: Our program states: priority for residents with local ties for social rental and affordable ownership, preferably even in their own neighborhood. Local housing is primarily for our own community.

118223.explanation: We facilitate the transformation of vacancy into housing by cutting rules, not by new subsidies. The market must do the work; the municipality must remove obstacles so developers can create housing quickly and efficiently.

118227.explanation: Instead of raising the income limit and making more people dependent on the government, FVD Lelystad focuses on the core: tax relief for everyone. By lowering property tax and local levies, residents keep more money in their own wallets.

118228.explanation: We want to invest in prevention by supporting neighborhood centers and promoting sports, part-time jobs, and internships. Youth work offers perspective and prevents nuisance. We cut spending on bureaucracy and climate policy, not on the future of our youth.

118236.explanation: In healthcare, human contact must be central, not the lowest price. We want care that is local, accessible, and focused on immediate help. Public money should go to providing care, not to bureaucracy or price-cutting.

118237.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality. We want a lean government that focuses on safety, infrastructure, and lower taxes. No new subsidy streams or symbolic politics; residents should be able to control their spending through lower taxes.

118239.explanation: Our program states explicitly: we will abolish the dog tax. It is an unnecessary and unfair levy that needlessly increases the burden on residents. We choose tax relief and a lean government.

118240.explanation: FVD Lelystad does not want new levies or taxes. Higher tourist taxes harm the local economy and entrepreneurs. We want lower burdens and an attractive climate for visitors and businesses.

118242.explanation: We stand for entrepreneurial freedom and a vibrant local economy. Shopkeepers should be able to decide for themselves when to open without bureaucratic obstacles. Fewer rules and more space for retailers ensures a lively and prosperous Lelystad.

118245.explanation: Our program states: no large-scale solar fields-they take up agricultural land and nature, cause nuisance, and destroy value. Agricultural land is for food production and our farmers, not for unprofitable climate projects.

118246.explanation: Culture valued by our residents deserves a place, but we are critical of spending. Subsidies must go to demonstrable social or economic value, not left-wing activist projects. Agora must offer a widely supported, neutral program.

118247.explanation: Sport is essential, but FVD stands for responsible finances. We will stop million-euro prestige projects. A new swimming pool is only possible if it is financially feasible and does not lead to higher burdens for citizens. First, get the basics in order.

118248.explanation: Sport and health are essential for our youth. We support clubs and initiatives that contribute to children's development. School swimming is a basic skill that enhances safety and social cohesion in our water-rich municipality.

118249.explanation: We stand for pragmatic policy. Instead of bans that lead to illegal street trading and crime, we choose regulation and enforcement. This way we maintain control over safety and livability in our neighborhoods.

118250.explanation: FVD Lelystad does not want rigid quotas that suffocate the housing market. We mainly build for starters, families, and middle incomes to make home ownership attainable again. Social rental housing must remain a safety net, not permanent dependence due to imposed percentages.

118253.explanation: Our program states clearly: we will abolish paid parking throughout the city and maintain sufficient parking space. A car is often a necessity; more expensive parking is a punishment for residents and entrepreneurs and empties the city center.

118254.explanation: Our program states: we maintain sufficient parking space and oppose reducing parking standards. The car is a necessity, not a luxury. We want good accessibility and will abolish paid parking throughout the city.

118255.explanation: Mobility is freedom. We oppose closing roads and discouraging car ownership. A car-free center harms accessibility and local retailers. We choose good connectivity and free parking.

118256.explanation: Our program states explicitly: Lelystad Airport open. We actively support opening it for holiday flights. This strengthens accessibility, the economy, and employment in our region.

118257.explanation: We are aware of the negative effects of large-scale housing of migrant workers. This leads to displacement in the housing market and pressure on facilities. We want to preserve social cohesion and give priority to our own residents.

118197.explanation: The CDA believes that elected representatives can properly weigh the interests of Lelystad residents. The CDA is not in favor of holding referenda. They increase and sharpen societal divisions rather than expanding support for proposals.

118198.explanation: CDA Lelystad welcomes the involvement and ownership of residents in their neighborhoods. For smaller tasks, management can be placed with individual residents. However, the municipality must provide residents with sufficient resources.

118200.explanation: In the interest of residents near hospitality venues, the CDA wants regulated opening hours.

118202.explanation: There are multiple shelters across the Netherlands for housing rejected asylum seekers. These shelters provide the municipality of Lelystad with options for accommodating rejected asylum seekers.

118203.explanation: This is already the current practice in Lelystad. Note that this policy applies to trees on municipal land, not to residents' own trees.

118204.explanation: The condition is that residents follow the burning guidelines and burning alerts and only burn under appropriate weather conditions. Using open fireplaces and wood stoves can have a significant impact on people with, for example, lung problems.

118209.explanation: It is the responsibility of entrepreneurs themselves to make and keep their buildings more accessible.

118215.explanation: The CDA finds safety and proper enforcement important. For this, enforcement officers are needed on the streets. As our city grows, more money must be allocated to enforcement in the coming years.

118217.explanation: When domestic violence or child abuse occurs, we want to focus on direct and rapid support for victims.

118219.explanation: Due to high housing prices, it is difficult for starters to buy a home. We therefore want to support them in purchasing their first house.

118220.explanation: We want to build so much that needs are met beyond just those of Lelystad. If insufficient building occurs, we will prioritize our own residents.

118223.explanation: Responsibility for converting lies with the owners of the vacant offices. The municipality can facilitate and consider solutions to realize conversions, but it does not need to provide subsidies.

118227.explanation: We find poverty reduction very important. At the same time, the current income limit is appropriate. It is more important to get residents into work.

118228.explanation: The CDA considers youth work relevant because it is important that all young people can fully participate in our society.

118236.explanation: The CDA considers it of utmost importance that children/youth receive appropriate help. We choose a balanced ratio between cost and quality of care.

118237.explanation: It is important that everyone has access to menstrual products. We want to continue current initiatives, for example at schools, where free products are provided.

118239.explanation: Although the revenue formally goes to general funds, the CDA favors specific allocation of proceeds for, for example, maintenance of dog walking areas and dog play fields.

118240.explanation: Lelystad should be attractive to tourists, so the CDA wants to keep the tourist tax at its current level.

118242.explanation: Lelystad has sufficient shopping Sundays. We also grant (small) entrepreneurs rest and time with their family, friends, and relatives.

118245.explanation: Because the agricultural land is of very good quality, the CDA does not want to cooperate with solar parks that sacrifice agricultural land.

118246.explanation: We choose for a lively city center with theater de Agora. Without the municipality's contribution, the Agora will have to close its doors.

118247.explanation: The current swimming pool in the Koploper is sufficient for now. Spending money on a new swimming pool is therefore not necessary.

118248.explanation: The CDA finds it important that children learn to swim, but we do not want to burden teachers with extra tasks. We therefore want to maintain the current approach. Parents lacking sufficient financial means can apply for a subsidy scheme.

118249.explanation: Research shows that using soft drugs can lead to health complaints. The CDA is therefore not in favor of the use and sale of soft drugs. We oppose a second coffee shop.

118250.explanation: There must be both affordable owner-occupied homes and social rental homes added. We opt for 30% social rental housing in this.

118253.explanation: CDA Lelystad considers it normal that users pay for the use of parking facilities. Currently, parking fees are approximately cost-covering, so an increase is not necessary.

118254.explanation: Because extra homes are being built in the center, it is also necessary to add extra parking spaces. This prevents future nuisance in surrounding neighborhoods or illegal parking.

118255.explanation: The aim is for cars to drive around the center and park in parking garages and parking lots on the edge. Residents of the Stadshart should still be able to access their homes.

118256.explanation: The CDA sees the upcoming opening of Lelystad Airport for holiday flights as an economic stimulus for Lelystad. Its arrival will bring new businesses that settle around the airport, creating extra jobs.

118257.explanation: The accommodation of labor migrants in large-scale locations on the edge of the city is an important measure against the nuisance many residents currently experience in their neighborhoods.

118197.explanation: It is important that residents of Lelystad can express their opinions. An advisory referendum can be held for important decisions, such as large construction projects. We also enable neighborhood referenda and explore the possibilities of a Citizens' Assembly.

118198.explanation: We strengthen local initiatives and the formation of resident groups. These are actively involved in decisions about the neighborhood and can participate in implementation and maintenance through the right to challenge.

118200.explanation: Personal freedom and self-regulation are very important to us, but they must not come at the expense of livability, for example for local residents. We support creating policies on this by engaging in dialogue with entrepreneurs and stakeholders.

118202.explanation: Lelystad will be a place where all people, regardless of residency status or papers, have the right to humane treatment, safe housing, protection against premature or unlawful expulsions, and against immigration policies that violate human rights.

118203.explanation: Stop cutting trees! There will be a replanting obligation for trees to be cut down. Where a tree cannot remain, compensation will be chosen based on crown volume and ecological value.

118204.explanation: Everyone has the right to clean air. Lelystad will completely ban wood burning, both indoors and outdoors. Wood smoke produces enormous amounts of fine particulate matter and causes health problems for many residents.

118209.explanation: In an inclusive society, all residents must be able to participate, regardless of their background, disability, chronic illness, neurodivergence, financial situation, or age.

118215.explanation: Lelystad invests in more street workers, neighborhood parents, and community officers-the eyes and ears of the neighborhood. Thanks to their efforts, potential problems in the neighborhood can be detected early before they escalate.

118217.explanation: It must become easier for victims of domestic violence to report and use crisis shelter when it is not possible to make the home situation sufficiently safe. Sufficient crisis accommodations are available in the region for these situations.

118219.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to help starters because housing is a basic right. However, the starter loan is not the right instrument, as it inflates prices and saddles people with higher debts. The municipality must specifically build affordably for this group.

118220.explanation: Everyone deserves an affordable home, regardless of your current housing situation.

118223.explanation: We favor converting office buildings, tackling vacancy, and stimulating housing turnover, but not through direct subsidies.

118227.explanation: Lelystad must extend minimum policy to incomes up to 130% of the social minimum. This includes, for example, special social assistance and collective health insurance.

118228.explanation: It is important to focus on a safe living environment, and youth workers play a very important role in this. They can speak in an accessible way about topics like drugs, weapons, (cyber)bullying, and (online) sexual abuse and exploitation. This can prevent much suffering.

118236.explanation: Vulnerable youth must never become victims of the overload on youth care. Quality and appropriate care must be paramount.

118237.explanation: Period poverty is a major problem and can lead to absenteeism at school or work. Menstrual products are a basic need. Distributing free products such as tampons, (reusable) pads, menstrual cups, and underwear helps with this.

118239.explanation: Dog tax revenues are spent on matters unrelated to dogs. Moreover, the dog tax distinguishes between owners of dogs and other pets. That distinction is not based on any justification.

118240.explanation: Tourism causes significant damage to the climate and nature due to associated polluting transport and overconsumption. Therefore, the municipality uses tourist tax revenues to promote sustainability and strengthen nature to limit tourism's damage.

118242.explanation: Personal freedom and self-regulation are very important to us, but at the same time, we oppose the 24-hour economy in which we consume more and more. We support creating policies on this by engaging in dialogue with entrepreneurs and residents.

118245.explanation: There will be no solar parks in nature. The installation of solar panels uses a solar ladder, which determines the order of installation. Agricultural land is not intended for solar meadows but for plant-based food production, nature development, and housing.

118246.explanation: We want to increase the budget for art, culture, and heritage. At the same time, cultural budgets must also provide space for accessible culture and small-scale organizations, especially in the neighborhoods. We will therefore carefully consider the distribution.

118247.explanation: We are open to exploring the possibilities for an (outdoor) swimming pool. Proper research is needed to map out where the demand lies.

118248.explanation: The municipality invests in sports, swimming lessons, culture, music lessons, and (independent) nature and sustainability education in schools. It is important that every child learns to swim, and school swimming provides equal opportunities for everyone.

118249.explanation: We advocate that no new coffeeshops may be established near schools.

118250.explanation: Everyone has the right to affordable housing. The Party for the Animals advocates for at least 40% of rental homes to be social rental.

118253.explanation: We want to encourage people to come to shopping centers on foot, by bike, or by public transport. We ensure a sufficient supply of, among other things, bicycle parking and good public transport. Raising the parking fee also belongs to this.

118254.explanation: We must handle scarce space carefully and counteract paving over. Existing parking lots will be converted into 'green' parking spaces using grass concrete tiles.

118255.explanation: Lelystad chooses, where possible, for a car-free city center. Visitors to the city center are encouraged to come by bike or public transport.

118256.explanation: Lelystad Airport will not be opened for civil aviation, and Lelystad will speak out for downsizing and against nuisance from aircraft traffic within the municipal boundaries.

118257.explanation: Labor migrants often work under and live in substandard conditions. The way labor migrants are housed must be strictly and frequently inspected, and good housing must be provided.

118197.explanation: NL Lelystad is based on the democratic mandate granted by elections. Referenda do not fit there. Residents are actively involved through dialogue, participation, and the use of warm data alongside statistics.

118198.explanation: For NL Lelystad, the municipality remains responsible for the ownership and management of public facilities. There is room for initiative, use, and cooperation, but not for taking over responsibilities.

118200.explanation: NL Lelystad chooses clear and equal frameworks for opening hours. Within those frameworks, there must be room for entrepreneurs, with attention to livability, safety, and a level playing field.

118202.explanation: According to NL Lelystad, after the completion of procedures, responsibility lies with the national government. Structural accommodation of rejected asylum seekers does not fit within the local role and puts extra pressure on facilities.

118203.explanation: NL Lelystad values green and quality of life. Compensation is desirable, but a fixed one-to-one rule is not always feasible. Custom solutions and quality must lead.

118204.explanation: For NL Lelystad, health and air quality are important. At the same time, measures must remain proportional and enforceable. Targeted approaches to nuisance are more effective than generic bans.

118209.explanation: Accessibility is important. NL Lelystad chooses to encourage and facilitate entrepreneurs where it is effective, without defaulting to general subsidies. Custom solutions and personal responsibility are paramount.

118215.explanation: NL Lelystad does not primarily focus on numbers but on effectiveness. Safety requires visible presence, good cooperation with the police, and targeted deployment where problems occur. More enforcement officers is not an end in itself; it’s about what actually works in practice.

118217.explanation: NL Lelystad believes that people affected by domestic violence must be able to be safely accommodated. Protection and safety weigh more heavily than organizational or financial objections.

118219.explanation: NL Lelystad wants to give first-time homebuyers a fair chance in the housing market. Starter loans are a targeted instrument that promotes housing flow without disturbing the market.

118220.explanation: NL Lelystad opposes rigid priority rules based on place of residence. The housing shortage requires flow, customized solutions, and choices that work for the city as a whole. ‘Own municipality first’ sounds logical but does not solve the problem nor make the system fairer.

118223.explanation: NL Lelystad sees opportunities in transforming vacant real estate but does not automatically opt for subsidies. Conversion must primarily be the responsibility of owners and developers, with room for customization where demonstrably necessary.

118227.explanation: NL Lelystad does not automatically favor raising income thresholds. Help must go to people who truly need it. This requires targeted support and customization, not continuously broadening schemes.

118228.explanation: NL Lelystad sees youth work as an important investment in prevention, talent development, and social safety. Cuts postpone problems and make them more expensive later. Effectiveness and quality are leading, not cutting facilities.

118236.explanation: NL Lelystad believes that quality, continuity, and appropriate help must guide youth care. Price is a factor but must never be decisive if it comes at the expense of effectiveness and youth interests.

118237.explanation: Nothing is free. Costs are always borne by the community. NL Lelystad chooses careful and purposeful policies, not giving away products for free without clear boundaries or necessity.

118239.explanation: NL Lelystad is critical about collecting dog tax due to its origin. Abolishing it is only possible if responsible coverage is provided. Policy choices must be explainable and financially sustainable.

118240.explanation: NL Lelystad does not choose to raise levies as a standard solution (except for inflation). Tourist tax must remain reasonable and proportional so that visitors and entrepreneurs are not discouraged and Lelystad remains attractive.

118242.explanation: NL Lelystad believes entrepreneurs should have room to determine their opening hours within clear and equal frameworks. More shopping Sundays can contribute to liveliness and economic vitality, provided livability and support are taken into account.

118245.explanation: NL Lelystad does not opt for a generic ban. Solar energy is needed but requires careful spatial consideration. Each location must be assessed for suitability, prioritizing roofs and existing buildings while respecting landscape and area function.

118246.explanation: NL Lelystad sees culture as part of a vibrant city. Support requires clear agreements on performance, audience reach, and financial responsibility. Ending subsidies is not a solution; steering for effectiveness is.

118247.explanation: NL Lelystad believes sports and swimming facilities must be well accessible and future-proof. A new swimming pool requires careful consideration of need, location, costs, and operation. This requires insight first, not an automatic choice for just any location.

118248.explanation: NL Lelystad recognizes the importance of a swimming diploma in a water-rich country like the Netherlands and particularly in a water-rich city like Lelystad. At the same time, this is not automatically a municipal task. Arrangements already exist for families and children who need it.

118249.explanation: NL Lelystad does not fundamentally oppose entrepreneurship. The establishment of coffeeshops must take place within clear rules, with attention to safety, livability, and enforceability. And within clear frameworks. Appropriate for a growing city.

118250.explanation: NL Lelystad does not choose fixed quotas. The housing construction task requires the right mix per location and target group. First-time buyers, families, and seniors must be able to progress.

118253.explanation: NL Lelystad does not support raising parking fees (except for inflation). Parking must remain accessible to visitors and entrepreneurs. Rates must be reasonable and contribute to accessibility, not drive away the public.

118254.explanation: NL Lelystad chooses not a simple 'more or less,' but a good balance. The center must remain accessible to visitors and entrepreneurs, while space is also needed for stay, greenery, and development. This requires customized solutions.

118255.explanation: NL Lelystad does not opt for a car-free center as a starting point. The center must remain accessible to residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs regardless of mode of transport. Traffic measures can contribute to livability. This calls for customization instead of a ban.

118256.explanation: Opening Lelystad Airport contributes to the city’s accessibility. NL Lelystad sees the airport as part of the regional infrastructure.

118257.explanation: NL Lelystad stands for decent housing for labor migrants, with attention to neighborhoods. Initiative and responsibility lie with employers and other parties. The municipality sets frameworks and supervises livability and compliance.

statements.title: Lelystad should build a new swimming pool in the Zuiderhage district.
The municipality should cut spending on youth work.

titleParts.glossary: People and organizations that help young people in the municipality with various things.

statements.title: For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: Lelystad should provide shelter to rejected asylum seekers.

titleParts.text: Lelystad moet opvang bieden aan afgewezen

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
Lelystad should stop providing funding to Theater Agora.
Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: In Lelystad the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Lelystad moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
The municipality should allow large housing locations for migrant workers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet grote woonlocaties voor

titleParts.glossary: People from another country who work temporarily in the Netherlands.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should prevent the opening of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The residents of Lelystad should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Lelystad moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: In Lelystad there should be more municipal enforcement officers (BOA’s) who deal with safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: At least 30% of the newly built homes in Lelystad should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands currently 28% of homes are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Lelystad moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Paid parking in the city center should become more expensive.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: Lelystad should establish a women's shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The center of Lelystad should become car-free.
Holiday flights should also operate from Lelystad Airport.
